page: Microsoft Azure L80as_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Lasv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz, delivering 80 vCPUs across 40 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 640.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Storage capabilities include a substantial 19,199 GB of local storage, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Benchmark data shows strong multi-core and computational performance. The server achieved top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core tests, software compilation with Clang, and ray tracing. Passmark CPU and encryption tests also ranked in the top tier. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance, along with memory bandwidth read and write operations, scored in the top 25%. However, single-threaded operations like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression fell into the average category. Passmark memory scores also registered as average.

This server is configured for data-intensive workloads requiring large local storage and high memory capacity. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and 19.2 TB local storage make it suitable for large-scale database operations, which is supported by its strong scores in Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations. It also demonstrates strong capabilities for local LLM inference on small to medium models, though performance drops to average on large 70B models. The lack of dedicated GPU hardware limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, but its high core count and memory density support heavy concurrent processing, software compilation, and web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure L80as_v4 is an x86_64 server in the Lasv4 family powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 80 vCPUs across 40 physical cores at 3.7 GHz, paired with 640.0 GB of memory and 19,199 GB of local storage. This instance lacks dedicated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in multi-core processing, software compilation, and encryption, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database operations. Conversely, single-threaded compression and memory scores remain in the average tier. Given its high resource density and massive local storage capacity, this server is designed for large database deployments, concurrent software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L80as_v4 is an x86_64 server featuring 80 vCPUs, 640 GB RAM, and 19,199 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L80as_v4 features 80 vCPUs, 640 GB RAM, and 19.2 TB local storage, optimized for database and multi-core workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity x86 server combining eighty AMD EPYC vCPUs, substantial memory, and nineteen terabytes of local storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 80 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
640.0 GB of system memory and 19,199 GB of local storage
Top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, and encryption
Strong database execution speeds and high memory bandwidth
Optimized for large databases, heavy concurrent processing, and medium LLM inference
